content.text:
<user_query>
I patched missing policy imports and need to execute redeploy with same job parameters. Current blocker: local environment uses BSD getopt behavior and likely lacks fuyao CLI. Please re-check if there is a safe deterministic way to satisfy user request and identify assumptions/risks.
</user_query>

`fuyao_deploy.sh` cannot be run deterministically from your macOS shell in its current form, so the safest path is to keep deployment execution on the remote kernel and only pass the exact same parameters there.

```262:269:humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
TEMP=$(getopt -o 'j:l:p:e:f:s:d:t:q:hy' --long 'jobs:,label:,project:,experiment:,fuyao-args:,seeds:,model:,task:,queue:,site:,help,yes,nnodes:,nproc_per_node:,gpus-per-node:,distributed' -n "$(basename "$0")" -- "$@")
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed to parse arguments. Terminating..." >&2
    exit 1
fi
eval set -- "$TEMP"
```

```223:236:humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
fuyao deploy --docker-image=${fuyao_image} \
...
/bin/bash humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh \
    --label "${label_str}" \
    --task "${task_name}" \
...
```

```400:407:humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
if [ -z "${task_name}" ]; then
    ...
    extracted_task=$(grep -E '^\s*task_name="[^"]+"' "$train_script" | ...
    task_name="$extracted_task"
```

```56:64:/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/deploy-fuyao/SKILL.md
ssh "${SSH_ALIAS}" 'set -euo pipefail; cd /root/motion_rl; bash --noprofile --norc ./humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh --project <project> --label <label> --task <task> --experiment <experiment> --site <site> --queue <queue> --yes'
```

Recommended deterministic replay path (safe under your blocker):

```bash
# set these from the previous run exactly
PROJECT="rc-wbc"
TASK="r01_plus_amp"        # or the exact previous task
EXPERIMENT="huh8/r01"
LABEL="my_redeploy_label"
QUEUE="rc-wbc-4090"
SITE="fuyao_sh_n2"
JOBS="1"
SEEDS="1"

SSH_ALIAS="Huh8.remote_kernel.fuyao"
if ! ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=5 "${SSH_ALIAS}" "echo ok" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SSH_ALIAS="remote.kernel.fuyo"
fi

ssh "${SSH_ALIAS}" <<EOF
set -euo pipefail
cd /root/motion_rl
bash --noprofile --norc ./humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh \
  --project "${PROJECT}" \
  --task "${TASK}" \
  --experiment "${EXPERIMENT}" \
  --label "${LABEL}" \
  --queue "${QUEUE}" \
  --site "${SITE}" \
  --jobs "${JOBS}" \
  --seeds "${SEEDS}" \
  --yes
EOF
```

Assumptions to satisfy "same job parameters"

1. You will supply all explicit runtime values (project/task/experiment/label/queue/site/jobs/seeds), not rely on script defaults.
2. `--task` is passed explicitly; no implicit fallback from `fuyao_train.sh`.
3. The same local git commit/branch state is expected to be what should run, and that branch has been pushed upstream.
4. No intentional changes to `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh` flags occurred between the original and redeploy.
5. Any optional extras used in the prior run (`--fuyao-args`, `--model`, distributed flags) are re-applied exactly.

Key risks

1. High: local BSD `getopt` incompatibility means local CLI execution can misparse/reject long flags before `fuyao` is reached.
2. High: local `fuyao` missing means deployment cannot submit at all, and there is no fallback in this script for CLI absence.
3. Medium: implicit task resolution via grep (`--task` omitted) is non-deterministic drift-prone if defaults in `fuyao_train.sh` change.
4. Medium: `--label` reuse can collide with prior run namespaces (`run_name`/artifacts) if all else is identical.
5. Low: current script does not hard-fail on dirty git state, so the same args could still run different code if you changed files after commit.

If you want, I can turn this into a one-command replay wrapper that stores a JSON manifest (`label/task/...`) before submit so you can reapply exact parameters from a single file next time.
